files.patch:
@@ -469,7 +469,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-min","title":"Create multi-platform installer script","description":"# Multi-Platform Installer Script

## Purpose
Enable one-liner installation of br on Linux, macOS, and Windows with automatic platform detection, checksum verification, and idempotent behavior.

## Technical Requirements

### Script Features
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Architecture detection (x86_64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism for concurrent protection
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection
- Proxy support (HTTPS_PROXY)

### Installation Methods
```bash
# Primary: curl
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# Alternative: wget
wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# With options
curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix=~/.local --no-modify-path
```

### Script Structure
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
REPO="Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust"
BINARY_NAME="br"
INSTALL_DIR="${HOME}/.local/bin"

# Functions
detect_platform()     # linux_amd64, darwin_arm64, etc.
detect_shell()        # bash, zsh, fish
download_release()    # curl with retry
verify_checksum()     # sha256sum verification
install_binary()      # atomic install
modify_path()         # idempotent PATH update
build_from_source()   # fallback with Rust install

# Main
main() {
    parse_args "$@"
    acquire_lock
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
    
    if \! download_release "$PLATFORM"; then
        warn "Binary not available, building from source..."
        build_from_source
    fi
    
    verify_checksum
    install_binary
    [[ "$MODIFY_PATH" == "true" ]] \u0026\u0026 modify_path
    
    echo "✓ br installed successfully\!"
    echo "  Run 'br --help' to get started."
}
```

### Checksum Verification
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local expected="$(curl -fsSL "${RELEASE_URL}.sha256")"
    local actual="$(sha256sum "$DOWNLOAD_PATH" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
    
    if [[ "$expected" \!= "$actual" ]]; then
        error "Checksum mismatch\! Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

### Idempotency Patterns
- mkdir -p (creates if not exists)
- Check before PATH modify (grep for existing entry)
- Lock file with stale detection
- Atomic moves (mv, not cp)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS 13+, Windows 11 (WSL)
- [ ] Detects x86_64 and arm64 architectures
- [ ] Verifies checksums before install
- [ ] Falls back to source build gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent (multiple runs don't break)
- [ ] Modifies PATH correctly for bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] Works behind HTTPS_PROXY
- [ ] Clear error messages on failure

## Files to Create
- `install.sh` - Main installer script
- `scripts/build-release.sh` - Build script for releases
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Release automation

## References
- ACFS install.sh patterns
- self_update crate documentation
- Homebrew installer conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyBarn","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:10.392094598Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473288374Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473191582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-mk35","title":"Standardize MCP display limit constants across tools.rs and resources.rs","description":"PARENT TASK 4 of 7: The MCP module has an inconsistency in how display truncation limits are handled. src/mcp/prompts.rs correctly defines named constants (STATUS_REPORT_LABELS_LIMIT, BOTTLENECK_DISPLAY_LIMIT, QUICK_WINS_DISPLAY_LIMIT at lines 30-34). But src/mcp/tools.rs and src/mcp/resources.rs use bare .take(10) and .take(15) calls for the same purpose — capping how many items appear in MCP tool/resource responses.

Specific locations: tools.rs:1012 .take(10) for recent events, tools.rs:1950 .take(15) for top labels, tools.rs:1953 .take(10) for blocked issues, tools.rs:1960 .take(10) for ready issues, resources.rs:876 .take(15) for bottleneck issues. Also: list default limit 50 and max limit 500 at tools.rs:731-732 are bare numbers, duplicated with cli/commands/list.rs:76.

The prompts.rs pattern is the gold standard — extend it to tools.rs and resources.rs. Additionally, the 30-day stale threshold at resources.rs:765,803 and business logic thresholds (density 0.5/0.2, chain depth 5/2, fan-out >=3) at resources.rs:760,783-795 should be named constants.

WHAT "DONE" LOOKS LIKE: All .take(N) calls in tools.rs and resources.rs reference named constants. List default/max limits are named constants shared between MCP tools and CLI. Graph health thresholds are named constants with documenting comments explaining the interpretation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:39.995779163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:25.802276616Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:25.801779725Z","close_reason":"All MCP display limits standardized. tools.rs has 6 named constants. resources.rs has 7 named constants (display + graph health). CLI list.rs has coupling comment.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mk35","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-6z0w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:38.816650628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mk35","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-914w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:38.533736719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mk35","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-gjgz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:37.945159554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mk35","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-spvg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:38.242751580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":242,"issue_id":"beads_rust-mk35","author":"ubuntu","text":"REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION: See src/mcp/prompts.rs lines 30-34 for the gold-standard pattern. The constants are defined at module level, use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE with _LIMIT suffix, and have documenting comments explaining what they control. tools.rs and resources.rs should follow this exact convention.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-mr1q","title":"Consolidate sync module constants in sync/mod.rs","description":"PARENT TASK 3 of 7: The sync module (src/sync/mod.rs, ~250KB) contains several duplicated inline constants: (1) BufReader capacity 2*1024*1024 (2MB) appears at lines 544, 1140, and 2989 — three identical BufReader::with_capacity calls. (2) Estimated bytes per issue for Vec pre-allocation (500) appears at lines 2695 and 2988 as (file_size / 500). (3) Missing-IDs preview limit (10) appears at sync/mod.rs:1400 AND cli/commands/sync.rs:512,519 — duplicated across modules.

WHY THIS MATTERS: The 2MB BufReader capacity is a performance-tuning parameter. If profiling suggests a different value, it must be changed in 3 places currently. The estimated-bytes-per-issue heuristic affects memory pre-allocation during JSONL parsing — having it as a named const documents the assumption and makes it tunable. The missing-IDs preview limit is cross-module duplication (sync engine vs CLI command).

WHAT "DONE" LOOKS LIKE: Three new const items (JSONL_BUFREADER_CAPACITY, ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE, MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT). All inline uses replaced. The CLI sync command imports the preview limit from the sync module rather than redefining it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:24.292592369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:25.640558442Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:25.640119540Z","close_reason":"All 4 sync constants defined: JSONL_BUFREADER_CAPACITY, ESTIMATED_BYTES_PER_ISSUE, MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT (cross-module), HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["sync"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mr1q","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7le9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:36.754186026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mr1q","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-a3ui","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:37.646112052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mr1q","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-g5dr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:37.342649098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mr1q","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-vsx1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:37.038014008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-mxn0","title":"Extract hardcoded constants and address unfinished code","description":"EPIC: Comprehensive audit found numerous hardcoded magic numbers, duplicated inline constants, and deferred-implementation markers across the codebase. This epic tracks all remediation work.

Background: An exhaustive codebase audit on 2026-03-24 identified three categories of issues: (1) Hardcoded constants that should be named — numeric literals scattered inline instead of being defined as const items. The worst offender is the title max length 500, duplicated in 6+ locations across validation, MCP tools, SQL DDL, and description strings. (2) Duplicated constants across modules — the same value defined independently in multiple function bodies or files (transaction retry params in two scopes in sqlite.rs, BufReader capacity in 3 places in sync/mod.rs, default ID prefix br in 4 places). (3) For-now deferred implementations — three comments explicitly flag incomplete features.

Overarching goals: Single source of truth for every magic number via named const in one canonical location. Consistency with existing patterns (MAX_COMMENT_BODY_BYTES in validation/mod.rs, named constants in mcp/prompts.rs). No behavioral changes — purely refactoring. Clippy/fmt clean throughout.

Scope: ~7 parent tasks, ~25 subtasks. All work in src/ only.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:01:47.113166114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:10:43.459804357Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constants-audit","refactoring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-kf0f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:18.016048408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-mk35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:17.442283561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-mr1q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:17.154322697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-o5mo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:18.322584078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-o8d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:16.876239317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-pp55","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:17.728379351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-sip9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:16.586954083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":237,"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","author":"ubuntu","text":"AUDIT METHODOLOGY: Full codebase searched with ripgrep for (1) all numeric literals in logic positions, (2) all TODO/FIXME/HACK comments, (3) all 'will'/'would'/'for now'/'later'/'eventually'/'placeholder'/'temporary' in comments. Each finding was manually verified by reading surrounding context. False positives (match arms, boolean flags, obvious array indices) were excluded. The audit covered all 48 source files in src/ but excluded tests/ and target/.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:09Z"},{"id":238,"issue_id":"beads_rust-mxn0","author":"ubuntu","text":"EXECUTION ORDER: Tasks 1-6 (constants extraction) are pure refactoring with no behavioral changes. They can be parallelized across agents since they touch different files, EXCEPT: Task 1.6 and Task 4.1 both touch mcp/tools.rs (dependency added). Task 7 (deferred features) is independent and lower priority — these are feature requests, not refactoring.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:09Z"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-mzdz","title":"Benchmark Comparison Script: br vs bd Performance Suite","description":"# Benchmark Comparison Script: br vs bd Performance Suite

## Current Infrastructure
conformance.rs already includes:
- BenchmarkConfig (warmup_runs, timed_runs, outlier_threshold)
- TimingStats (mean, median, p95, std_dev, min, max)
- run_benchmark() function with outlier filtering
- Timing logged for each command execution

## Benchmarks to Implement
### Command Latency
- init (cold start)
- create single issue
- create 100 issues (throughput)
- list (10/100/1000 issues)
- search (various corpus sizes)
- sync flush/import

### Memory Usage
- Peak memory during large operations
- Memory after workspace with 1000 issues

### Expected Outcomes
Based on Rust vs Go:
- Lower latency (no GC pauses)
- Faster startup (native binary)
- Lower memory footprint (no runtime)

## Output Format
JSON report with:
- Command-by-command timing comparisons
- Statistical summaries
- Performance ratio (br time / bd time)","notes":"Added memory RSS comparisons for list_1000, sync --flush-only (1000 issues), and sync --import-only (1000 issues) using /usr/bin/time -v; memory section appended when RSS available.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkBrook","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:20.050147098Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:46.001377646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:46.001377646Z","close_reason":"Benchmark comparison suite implemented in tests/benchmark_comparison.rs (br vs bd latency + memory comparisons) and notes indicate RSS additions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mzdz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-8tki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mzdz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-d28m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mzdz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-m34c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-mzdz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-v740","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
